title: BWC Request: Deobra Redden — Man Vaults Over Judge's Bench and Attacks Her Mid-Sentencing

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

Public Records Request — Body-Worn Camera Footage: Deobra Redden

To Whom It May Concern,

Pursuant to the Nevada Public Records Act (NRS Chapter 239), I am requesting access to the following public records:

All body-worn camera (BWC) footage, dashcam footage, and any other audio or video recordings related to the following incident:

Case/Incident: Deobra Redden — Man Vaults Over Judge's Bench and Attacks Her Mid-Sentencing
Date of Incident: January 2, 2024
Agency: Clark County District Court
Summary: Las Vegas, Jan 3 2024. Deobra Redden, 30, being sentenced for felony battery. As Judge Mary Kay Holthus reads his criminal record, he vaults over the defense table and jumps the judge's bench, landing...


Note: I understand this footage has already been released to media outlets. I am requesting the same footage be provided directly to me as a member of the public under the applicable records law.

This request includes:
1. All body-worn camera footage from any officers involved in the incident
2. All dashcam / in-car camera footage from responding vehicles
3. Any booking room, interview room, or holding area footage related to this incident
4. Any associated arrest reports or incident reports (if not exempt)

I request that this footage be provided electronically if possible (via download link, USB, or email attachment).

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance. If any portion of this request is denied, please cite the specific statutory exemption being claimed.

Thank you for your assistance.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Sam O
